mission.title: cert-manager: Support scoped-API token based authentication with Cloudflare
mission.description: Support scoped-API token based authentication with Cloudflare. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Cloudflare recently released scoped api tokens [here](https://blog.cloudflare.com/api-tokens-general-availability/). And I would like to use that instead of the previous API Key

Just to be crystal clear, I will state here:
1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support scoped-API token based authentication with…
steps.description:
Support scoped-API token based authentication with Cloudflare
```yaml
0905 04:51:24.366740       1 base_controller.go:189] 
cert-manager/controller/challenges "msg"="re-queuing item  due to error processing" 
"error"="Cloudflare API Error
  Error:
    6003: Invalid request headers\u003c- 
    6103: Invalid format for X-Auth-Key header" "key"="xxxxxx"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support scoped-API token based authentication with…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Support scoped-API token based authentication with Cloudflare

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
